For readiness, use a specific score threshold (e.g., 80% or 4/5) and decide whether mastery requires repeated success on the same type or mixed problems. To create a final, unambiguous readiness rule: combine the 3-in-a-row and 4/5 mixed criteria with an explicit requirement for at least one mixed problem. To define readiness: combine 3-in-a-row correct (with no notes) and 4/5 on mixed problems, but require at least one mixed problem to be completed before declaring readiness. This rule is explicit and must be applied as a condition before any readiness is declared. To maintain pace during a busy week, create a simple, time-blocked daily plan that aligns with user's preferred sequential study structure (notes → hard types → mixed problems) and includes clear stopping rules.
To review assignments efficiently: check requirements first, then accuracy, then missing parts; if those are fine, treat the rest as cleanup and use a 2-minute checklist.
